div1.entryFree:
Notorious, 1) manifest to the world, known to everybody; in a bad sense: you have been a n. bawd, Meas. IV, 2, 14. a most n. pirate, IV, 3, 75. I would it were not n. All's I, 1, 41. Alençon, that n. Machiavel, H6A V, 4, 74. your goodness, since you provoke me, shall be most n. H8 III, 2, 288.
2) notable, egregious: thou foul abettor, thou n. bawd, Lucr. 886. bring in here two n. benefactors, Meas. II, 1, 50. to your n. shame, Err. IV, 1, 84. you n. villain, Shr. V, 1, 54. I know him a n. liar, All's I, 1, 111. you have done me wrong, n. wrong, Tw. V, 337. made the most n. geck and gull, Tw. V, 337 wherein I did not some n. ill, Tit. V, 1, 127. some base n. knave, Oth. IV, 2, 140. 'tis a n. villain, V, 2, 239.
